perturbation.original_text: 11. Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, t he Receiving Party undertakes to promptly return to the Disclosing Party, or, at the Disclosi ng Party’s discretion, destroy all the Confidential Information (together with all copies and extracts) stored by the Receiving Party and/or its representatives (including without limitation, on c omputer disks and/or other digital media). Within five (5) calendar days from the date of term ination (expiration) of this Agreement, unless there was a written request from the Disclosing Par ty, the Receiving Party shall destroy all Confidential Information (together with all copies and extracts) stored by the Receiving Party and/or its representatives (including without limit ation, on computer disks and/or other digital media). In case of destruction of Confidential Info rmation under the terms of this Section, the Receiving Party shall send to the Disclosing Party a written confirmation of such destruction, which should contain a list of the destroyed Confid ential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, t he Receiving Party undertakes to promptly return to the Disclosing Party, or, at the Disclosi ng Party’s discretion, destroy all the Confidential Information (together with all copies and extracts) stored by the Receiving Party and/or its representatives (including without limitation, on c omputer disks and/or other digital media). Within one (1) calendar day from the date of term ination (expiration) of this Agreement, unless there was a written request from the Disclosing Par ty, the Receiving Party shall destroy all Confidential Information (together with all copies and extracts) stored by the Receiving Party and/or its representatives (including without limit ation, on computer disks and/or other digital media). In case of destruction of Confidential Info rmation under the terms of this Section, the Receiving Party shall send to the Disclosing Party a written confirmation of such destruction, which should contain a list of the destroyed Confid ential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the destruction deadline to one (1) calendar day may not provide sufficient time, contradicting data disposal compliance requirements in some jurisdictions that might mandate a reasonable timeframe for secure data disposal, especially considering that the original text does not specify what happens if the destruction does not occur.
perturbation.contradicted_law: GDPR Article 17, Right to Erasure ('Right to be Forgotten')
perturbation.law_explanation: GDPR mandates data controllers to erase personal data without undue delay. While the modified text expedites destruction, a one-day deadline may not be sufficient to ensure secure and compliant data erasure, potentially violating the ‘undue delay’ requirement if complex or large datasets are involved, or the destruction is not properly documented.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Art. 17 GDPR – Right to erasure (‘right to be forgotten’) - General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Search for: Skip to content Search for: Imprint | Privacy Policy | Liability The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her without undue delay and the controller shall have the obligation to erase personal data without undue delay where one of the following grounds applies: the personal data are no longer necessary in relation to the purposes for which they were collected or otherwise processed; the data subject withdraws consent on which the processing is based according to point (a) of Article 6 (1), or point (a) of Article 9 (2), and where there is no other legal ground for the processing; the data subject objects to the processing pursuant to Article 21 (1) and there are no overriding legitimate grounds for the processing, or the data subject objects to the processing pursuant to Article 21 (2); the personal data have been unlawfully processed; the personal data have to be erased for compliance with a legal obligation in Union or Member State law to which the controller is subject; the personal data have been collected in relation to the offer of information society services referred to in Article 8 (1). Where the controller has made the personal data public and is obliged pursuant to paragraph 1 to erase the personal data, the controller, taking account of available technology and the cost of implementation, shall take reasonable steps, including technical measures, to inform controllers which are processing the personal data that the data subject has requested the erasure by such controllers of any links to, or copy or replication of, those personal data. Paragraphs 1 and 2 shall not apply to the extent that processing is necessary: for exercising the right of freedom of expression and information; for compliance with a legal obligation which requires processing by Union or Member State law to w
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the time frame for confidential information destruction from five days to one day. The Law Explanation notes that GDPR mandates data controllers to erase personal data without undue delay. Scraped Law Snippet 1 (Art. 17 GDPR) also states that the data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her without undue delay and the controller shall have the obligation to erase personal data without undue delay. The Perturbation Explanation specifies that this change may not provide sufficient time for secure data disposal. The one-day deadline could be considered an 'undue delay' if it doesn't allow for compliant data erasure, which violates GDPR. This creates a contradiction because the contract's timeframe potentially conflicts with the 'without undue delay' requirement of GDPR.

perturbation.original_text: 6. This Agreement shall enter into effect upon its exe cution and shall remain effective for three (3) years, except for the obligations of the Receiving Party as to preservation and nondisclosure of the Confidential Information set forth in Section 5 hereof above which remain in force after termination (or expiration) of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Agreement shall enter into effect upon its exe cution and shall remain effective for one (1) year, except for the obligations of the Receiving Party as to preservation and nondisclosure of the Confidential Information set forth in Section 5 hereof above which remain in force after termination (or expiration) of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the agreement's effective duration to one year, while keeping the confidentiality obligations indefinite, creates an inconsistency. This may conflict with trade secret laws, as some jurisdictions require active measures to protect trade secrets for them to remain enforceable, and a very short agreement duration could be interpreted as a lack of reasonable protection efforts.
perturbation.law_explanation: UTSA requires reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy for information to be protected as a trade secret. If the confidentiality agreement lasts only one year, with the obligation to keep secret information for much longer, it may be viewed as an unreasonable approach, undermining the efforts to enforce a long-term trade secret claim.

perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the agreement's effective duration to one year while maintaining indefinite confidentiality obligations. The Law Explanation states that under UTSA, 'reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy' are required for trade secret protection. The Perturbation Explanation elaborates that a very short agreement duration may be interpreted as a lack of reasonable protection efforts, which undermines the efforts to enforce a long-term trade secret claim. Although there are no helpful law snippets provided, the fact that the Uniform Law Commission addresses state laws and trade secrets in general suggests there may be an applicable law, and this agreement's change goes against the UTSA principle. This creates a clear contradiction as the agreement's short duration undermines the necessary 'reasonable efforts' to protect confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 16. All notices and documents sent by the Parties to ea ch other under this Agreement shall be made in writing, sealed (if applicable) by the respectiv e Party and delivered either by (i) courier and/or a representative of the Party concerned (in this ca se they are deemed received from the moment the representative of the Party to which such notic e/document was sent, confirms receipt of the notification/document with his signature, indicatin g the date and time of receipt), or (ii) insured letter by mail to the post address of the Party con cerned, as indicated at the end of this Agreement (in this case, such letters are considered to be re ceived after three (3) calendar days from the date of posting).
perturbation.changed_text: 16. All notices and documents sent by the Parties to ea ch other under this Agreement shall be made orally.
perturbation.explanation: Requiring all notices and documents to be sent 'orally' directly contradicts standard legal practice and the Statute of Frauds in many jurisdictions, which require certain contracts and related notices to be in writing to be enforceable. Eliminating the written requirement creates ambiguity and potential unenforceability.
perturbation.law_citation: Various state statutes, e.g., California Civil Code § 1624
perturbation.law_explanation: The Statute of Frauds requires certain contracts (and often modifications or notices related to them) to be in writing to be enforceable. Changing the notification method to oral only violates the Statute of Frauds because it eliminates the written form, creating uncertainty and unenforceability issues.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1624. skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 2. MANNER OF CREATING CONTRACTS [1619 - 1633] ( Title 2 enacted 1872. ) 1624. (a) The following contracts are invalid, unless they, or some note or memorandum thereof, are in writing and subscribed by the party to be charged or by the party’s agent: (1) An agreement that by its terms is not to be performed within a year from the making thereof. (2) A special promise to answer for the debt, default, or miscarriage of another, except in the cases provided for in Section 2794. (3) An agreement for the leasing for a longer period than one year, or for the sale of real property, or of an interest therein; such an agreement, if made by an agent of the party sought to be charged, is invalid, unless the authority of the agent is in writing, subscribed by the party sought to be charged. (4) An agreement authorizing or employing an agent, broker, or any other person to purchase or sell real estate, or to lease real estate for a longer period than one year, or to procure, introduce, or find a purchaser or seller of real estate or a lessee or lessor of real estate where the lease is for a longer period
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: statute of frauds | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you statute of frauds Statute of frauds is a statute requiring certain contracts to be in writing and signed by the parties bound by the contract. The purpose is to prevent fraud and other injury. The most common types of contracts to which the statute applies are contracts that involve the sale or transfer of land, (see: real estate transactions ), and contracts that cannot be completed within one year. See also: Y amaha Motor Corp., U.S.A. v. Calhoun , 516 U.S. 199 (1996) Contracts for the sale of goods worth $500 or more. UCC § 2-201 [Last reviewed in June of 2024 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE commercial activities finance financial services housing PROPERTY landlord & tenant property & real estate law business law contracts wex definitions business sectors commercial transactions property law statutes Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The changed text mandates oral notices, directly contradicting the Statute of Frauds, which requires certain contracts (or related notices) to be in writing to be enforceable. Scraped Law Snippet 1 (California Code, CIV 1624) specifies that certain agreements are invalid unless they, or some note or memorandum thereof, are in writing. Scraped Law Snippet 2 (statute of frauds | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute) explicitly states the statute of frauds is a statute requiring certain contracts to be in writing and signed by the parties bound by the contract. The modified text removes the written requirement, creating a clear contradiction. This alteration undermines the legal requirement for written documentation, leading to potential unenforceability, especially regarding contracts that cannot be completed within one year or involve the sale or transfer of land, as noted in Scraped Law Snippet 2.

perturbation.original_text: 12. All disputes which may arise from or in connection with this Agreement shall be settled through negotiations between the Parties. If such a dispute between the Parties cannot be settled through negotiations between the Parties, either Party may submit such a dispute to a court in accordance with [ specify the jurisdiction ] laws.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. All disputes which may arise from or in connection with this Agreement shall be settled through negotiations between the Parties. If such a dispute between the Parties cannot be settled through negotiations between the Parties, any dispute arising out of or relating to this agreement, or the breach thereof, shall be resolved exclusively by binding arbitration in North Korea.
perturbation.explanation: Specifying North Korea as the exclusive arbitration venue creates a legal contradiction, because enforcing arbitration decisions from North Korea would be problematic in most jurisdictions, especially given international sanctions and human rights concerns. This could be viewed as an unenforceable forum selection clause.
perturbation.law_explanation: While the Federal Arbitration Act favors arbitration, it also acknowledges international treaties and comity. Selecting North Korea as the sole arbitration venue undermines the enforceability of the agreement due to political and legal challenges and possible violation of public policy concerns, therefore preventing Section 201's intent.

Enforcement of Convention. 202. Agreement or award falling under the Convention. 203. Jurisdiction; amount in controversy. 204. Venue. 205. Removal of cases from State courts. 206. L. 91–368, §1, July 31, 1970, 84 Stat. 692 , added heading for chapter 2 and analysis of sections for such chapter. §201. Enforcement of Convention The Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Arbitral Awards of June 10, 1958, shall be enforced in United States courts in accordance with this chapter. (Added Pub. L. 91–368, §1, July 31, 1970, 84 Stat. 692 .) Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date Pub. L. 91–368, §4, July 31, 1970, 84 Stat. 693 , provided that: "This Act [enacting this chapter] shall be effective upon the entry into force of the Convention on Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Arbitral Awards with respect to the United States." The Convention entered into force for the United States on Dec. 29, 1970. §202. Agreement or award falling under the Convention An arbitration agreement or arbitral award arising out of a legal relationship, whether contractual or not, which is considered as commercial, including a transaction, contract, or agreement described in section 2 of this title , falls under the Convention. An agreement or award arising out of such a relationship which is entirely between citizens of the United States shall be deemed not to fall under the Convention unless that relationship involves property located abroad, envisages performance or enforcement abroad, or has some other reasonable relation with one or more foreign states. For the purpose of this section a corporation is a citizen of the United States i
perturbation.justification: The modified text designates North Korea as the exclusive arbitration venue. This contradicts the principle of enforceability of arbitration agreements, particularly foreign ones. Section 201 of the Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Arbitral Awards, as codified in 9 U.S.C. Ch. 2, aims to enforce foreign arbitral awards in United States courts. However, due to political and legal challenges, including potential sanctions and human rights concerns, enforcing an arbitration award from North Korea would be highly problematic and could be viewed as against public policy. The original text allowed for disputes to be submitted to a court in accordance with specified jurisdiction laws, while the changed text mandates exclusive arbitration in North Korea, thus hindering the enforceability of any arbitration award in the US under the Convention.
